label: Toxicity Filter
description: Filter compounds based on SMILES data and physicochemical thresholds.

defaultInputs.name: Input Compounds
defaultInputs.tooltip: Table of compounds including at least a SMILES column and basic properties.

defaultOutputs.name: SMILES
defaultOutputs.tooltip: SMILES string representing the compound's chemical structure.

defaultOutputs.name: Ames Mutagenicity
defaultOutputs.tooltip: Predicted probability of Ames mutagenicity (0–1). Higher values indicate greater mutagenic risk.

defaultOutputs.name: Hepatotoxicity
defaultOutputs.tooltip: Predicted probability of liver toxicity (0–1). Higher values indicate greater hepatotoxic risk.

defaultOutputs.name: Skin Sensitization
defaultOutputs.tooltip: Predicted probability of skin sensitization (0–1). Higher values indicate greater sensitization risk.

defaultOutputs.name: Status
defaultOutputs.tooltip: Overall toxicity decision for the compound (Pass, Conditional, or Fail).

configSchema.label: Model
configSchema.tooltip:
Choose which ADMET prediction model to use:

- **SwissADME**: Best for concept-stage screening, medicinal chemistry intuition, fast triage
- **ADMET-AI**: Best for predictive modeling, prioritization, portfolio ranking, data science pipelines
- **preADMET**: Best for cross-checking, academic comparability, legacy workflows

configSchema.label: Lipophilicity Range
configSchema.tooltip: Lipophilicity (logP) range predicted by ADMET-AI (min - max).

configSchema.label: Ames Toxicity Threshold
configSchema.tooltip: Acceptable probability range for Ames mutagenicity (0–1). Compounds with probability above the max will be flagged or excluded.

configSchema.label: Hepatotoxicity Threshold
configSchema.tooltip: Acceptable probability range for hepatotoxicity (0–1). Compounds with probability above the max will be flagged or excluded.

configSchema.label: Skin Sensitization Threshold
configSchema.tooltip: Acceptable probability range for skin sensitization (0–1). Compounds with probability above the max will be flagged or excluded.
